title: dysoxylum hamiltonii
intro: Dysoxylum hamiltonii, also known as the East Indian wrightia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dysoxylum hamiltonii include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include its leaves and bark being used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
